mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with trade-offs in complexity and resource requirements depending on the deployment scale.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.1

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the vLLM Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.19.1/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.1

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example vLLM Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to deploy a model using vLLM.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This command creates an example vLLM resource for a GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM instances in the 'vllm' namespace.
> This command lists the pods to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the vLLM instances and any associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This step upgrades the vLLM installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get vllms -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.1

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> Backup the current vLLM configuration.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify the upgrade by checking the status of the vLLM instances.
commands: kubectl get vllms -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm chart may not be installed or the repository is not added.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.1' and 'helm repo update'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The model specified in the Custom Resource may not be available or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the model name in the vLLM CR and ensure it is correct and available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the vLLM service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM service may not be running or the network policy is blocking access.

**Fix:**
Verify the vLLM service is running with 'kubectl get svc -n vllm' and check network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure the cluster has sufficient CPU and memory resources available for the vLLM deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM instances listed with 'kubectl get vllms -n vllm' and their status should be 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
